topic: Sharp partial identification of ACRT(d) in continuous-treatment difference-in-differences under structural cross-dose Lipschitz or monotone regularity of ATT(l|d) in the conditioning group d.

open_problems.open_problem: What is the sharp finite-grid ACRT(d) envelope in continuous-treatment DiD when cross-dose selection terms are constrained jointly by Lipschitz or isotonic regularity across conditioning doses?

source_refs.claim: Continuous-treatment DiD identifies treated-type parameters under generalized parallel trends, but cross-dose comparisons are challenging under treatment-effect heterogeneity and TWFE parameters can be hard to interpret.
source_refs.author: Brantly Callaway, Andrew Goodman-Bacon, Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: Monotone instrumental-variable and monotone treatment-response restrictions tighten otherwise weak nonparametric treatment-effect bounds.
source_refs.author: Charles F. Manski, John V. Pepper

source_refs.claim: Sharp bounds on partially identified parameters are often LP values, and LP structure can support estimation and inference.
source_refs.author: Andrei Voronin

source_refs.excerpt: the finite-grid sharp identified set for theta_tau,h is the projection of a nonempty compact polytope
the sharp identified set is the observed contrast plus the support interval of that signed box functional
the monotone-concave grid envelope is the projection of an explicitly described linear-inequality polytope

open_problems.why_unsolved: Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna leave the cross-dose ATT(l|d) comparisons exposed to unidentified selection-bias terms, while the shape-restriction literature gives monotone or LP-style bound templates outside this continuous-DiD decomposition. Prior CausalSmith artifacts have finite-grid LP and support-function ingredients, but none couples the CGS cross-dose nuisance coordinates into one Lipschitz or isotonic feasible set whose image is the ACRT(d) interval.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A sharpness theorem identifying the lower and upper ACRT(d) endpoints as the values of explicit finite-grid primal LPs, equivalently dual support functions, over a joint Lipschitz or isotonic ATT(l|d) nuisance polytope.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest motivation arc for continuous-treatment DiD, ACRT-style contrasts, and TWFE interpretation failures.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: continuous doses are common in DiD -> treated-type parameters are identifiable under generalized parallel trends -> cross-dose ACRT(d) comparisons need additional structure

method_exemplars.technique: Nonparametric bound tightening by imposing monotone instrumental-variable and monotone treatment-response restrictions, with sharp interval logic.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The proposed regularity constraints play the same role as credible shape restrictions that shrink an otherwise wide nonparametric treatment-effect set.

method_exemplars.technique: LP-value sharp-bound characterization plus root-n LP-value estimation under feasibility and solution multiplicity.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The ACRT(d) endpoints are exactly LP values once the finite dose grid and cross-dose inequalities are fixed.

open_problems.open_problem: How can one characterize the critical Lipschitz constant L*(d) at which zero first enters the sharp ACRT(d) identified interval?

source_refs.claim: Intersection bounds can be written as extrema over many inequalities or as linear programs with potentially infinite constraint sets, and plug-in analogs can be biased.
source_refs.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Sokbae Lee, Adam M. Rosen

source_refs.claim: Scalar interval-identified parameters need confidence intervals that remain valid and usable even when sample analog bounds are awkward or empty.
source_refs.author: Joerg Stoye

source_refs.excerpt: there is a critical radius rho* in (0,1) such that the lower endpoint is positive for rho<rho*
derive or conjecture an explicit formula for the critical radius rho* in the two-bin witness
the support value is min{rho_*,rho_0+rho_1}, so a positive observed FDID contrast can coexist with negative causal moderation

open_problems.why_unsolved: The web literature gives inference and optimization tools for interval endpoints at a fixed constraint set, and prior proposals identify analogous sensitivity-radius thresholds, but neither source derives the inverse sensitivity problem for continuous-treatment DiD: the smallest cross-dose regularity relaxation L for which the ACRT(d) sign becomes unidentified.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A parametric-LP characterization proving ACRT(d) endpoints are monotone piecewise-linear functions of L and expressing L*(d) by primal feasibility or dual certificate conditions, with a plug-in inference target for the threshold.

writing_exemplar.why: It frames bounds as optimized objects over inequality systems, which is the right setup for turning endpoint movement in L into a sensitivity frontier.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: bounds are extrema over restrictions -> naive endpoint estimates are nonregular -> ACRT(d) sign sensitivity asks where the optimized interval first crosses zero

method_exemplars.technique: Never-empty confidence intervals for scalar interval-identified parameters with asymptotically normal bound estimators.
method_exemplars.why_closest: L*(d) is a scalar functional generated by interval endpoints, and its inference inherits endpoint nonregularity.

method_exemplars.technique: Optimal inference for linear functionals over convex smoothness and shape classes.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A Lipschitz nuisance class is a convex shape class, and the ACRT(d) endpoint is a linear functional optimized over it.

open_problems.open_problem: Can one exhibit a nonseparability witness showing that joint Lipschitz or isotonic cross-dose constraints strictly sharpen ACRT(d) bounds beyond independently bounding each selection-bias term?

source_refs.claim: Nonseparable panel models can make usual linear fixed-effect estimands miss the identified average effect, motivating partial-identification analysis.
source_refs.author: Victor Chernozhukov, Ivan Fernandez-Val, Jinyong Hahn, Whitney Newey

source_refs.claim: Monotonicity and concavity can provide identifying power in nonseparable models when point-identification conditions fail.
source_refs.author: Takuya Ishihara

source_refs.excerpt: the one-dose baseline-rank-slice marginal sensitivity interval contains zero, while the sharp rank-slice interval is strictly positive
sharp finite LP envelope for adjacent-rank dose-response slope under continuous IV with quantile-copula tube
pointwise sharp sensitivity intervals are imported as a primitive rather than derived

open_problems.why_unsolved: Lipschitz and isotonic restrictions couple nuisance coordinates across conditioning groups, so the endpoint-attaining values for separate cross-dose terms may be mutually infeasible. Prior CausalSmith rank-coupling artifacts already found that marginal intervals can miss joint sign information, but no continuous-DiD construction shows this phenomenon for ACRT(d).
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A minimal two- or three-dose finite observed law with computed marginal product bounds and joint Lipschitz/isotonic LP bounds, plus a primal infeasibility or dual certificate proving strict sharpening.

writing_exemplar.why: It is a clear template for explaining why nonseparability changes what panel estimands can reveal.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: panel causal effects can be nonseparable -> linear estimands lose direct causal meaning -> shape-restricted partial identification can recover sharper target information

method_exemplars.technique: Partial identification in nonseparable models using monotonicity and concavity restrictions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The witness needs to show how cross-coordinate shape restrictions identify more than coordinatewise intervals.

method_exemplars.technique: Primal-dual LP value analysis under solution multiplicity.
method_exemplars.why_closest: A nonseparability witness can be certified by comparing product-box LP values with coupled-constraint LP values.

open_problems.open_problem: How should plug-in inference be conducted for Lipschitz or isotonic ACRT(d) envelope endpoints and L*(d) when active constraints change or the sample analog feasible set is empty?

source_refs.claim: Subvector inference in partially identified models with many moment inequalities can be uniformly valid without Donsker weak convergence.
source_refs.author: Alexandre Belloni, Federico Bugni, Victor Chernozhukov

source_refs.claim: Calibrated projection builds confidence intervals for functions of partially identified parameters by extremizing over relaxed sample moment inequalities.
source_refs.author: Hiroaki Kaido, Francesca Molinari, Joerg Stoye

source_refs.claim: Inference for interval-identified scalar parameters should remain nonempty and valid when the sample analog set is problematic.
source_refs.author: Joerg Stoye

source_refs.excerpt: confidence sets for Theta_a(B) that are pointwise asymptotically valid
the plug-in statistic Lambda_hat_h is root-N asymptotically normal away from the equality frontier
The fixed-phi interval inference conjecture is open because it depends on the exact interval critical-value calibration

open_problems.why_unsolved: The ACRT(d) endpoints are nonsmooth optimized functions of first-stage DiD estimates and shape constraints. Existing inference papers cover many moment inequalities, projections, and scalar intervals, while prior CausalSmith artifacts cover endpoint or frontier inference separately; none handles the combined finite-grid Lipschitz/isotonic LP, active-face switching, empty sample feasibility, and zero-entry threshold in one ACRT(d) setting.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A uniformly valid confidence procedure for ACRT(d) lower and upper endpoints, and for L*(d), using relaxed LP constraints, bootstrap or Gaussian approximations, and explicit treatment of active-set ties and empty sample analog identified sets.

writing_exemplar.why: It gives a concise framing for practical inference on scalar interval-identified parameters when sample analog intervals can be pathological.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: identified sets are intervals -> sample bounds can be empty or awkward -> ACRT(d) endpoint inference needs valid intervals that remain usable at boundaries

method_exemplars.technique: MinMax tests and bootstrap or analytical critical values for many moment inequalities with uniform size control.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The finite-grid Lipschitz/isotonic ACRT(d) set is represented by many equality and inequality restrictions.

method_exemplars.technique: Calibrated projection confidence intervals from optimization over relaxed sample analog moment constraints.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The ACRT(d) endpoint is a low-dimensional function of a partially identified finite-dimensional nuisance vector.

open_problems.open_problem: Can continuous-treatment TWFE level-weight decompositions be embedded as linear restrictions or diagnostics on the same Lipschitz/isotonic nuisance set that bounds ACRT(d)?

source_refs.claim: Continuous-treatment TWFE parameters can be hard to interpret even with two periods because heterogeneous effects create nontransparent decompositions.
source_refs.author: Brantly Callaway, Andrew Goodman-Bacon, Pedro H. C. Sant'Anna

source_refs.claim: TWFE coefficients with heterogeneous treatment effects can be weighted sums with nonconvex weights or contamination from other effects.
source_refs.author: Clement de Chaisemartin, Xavier D'Haultfoeuille

source_refs.excerpt: LP-DiD and BJS weights are equal if and only if C_h(g;pi) is constant over positive-mass eligible cohorts
Conjectures timing-frontier--plugin-diagnostic are not yet proved
GoodmanBacon2021 decomposes TWFE timing weights, not LP-DiD versus imputation weights

open_problems.why_unsolved: TWFE decomposition papers diagnose regression weights, while partial-ID bounds optimize the target over a feasible causal-effect array. The prior LP-DiD/BJS frontier is a discrete staggered-adoption weight problem; continuous-dose ACRT(d) introduces dose-indexed cross-dose terms and regularity coupling, so the equality, drift, and diagnostic conditions do not transfer directly.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A finite-grid representation writing TWFE and ACRT(d) as linear functionals of the same ATT(l|d) array, followed by an iff frontier and signed-drift formula under the Lipschitz/isotonic feasible set.

writing_exemplar.why: It is a strong template for motivating regression-weight decompositions as a diagnostic rather than a target.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: TWFE is widely used -> heterogeneous effects make weights hard to interpret -> continuous-dose ACRT(d) needs a target-specific decomposition and diagnostic

method_exemplars.technique: Continuous-treatment DiD decomposition and TWFE level-weight formulas.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The nuisance array and ACRT(d) target are inherited from this continuous-dose DiD framework.

method_exemplars.technique: Switcher-based estimators for treatments continuously distributed at every period under placebo-testable parallel trends.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It gives an adjacent continuous-treatment DiD estimation target whose weights can be compared to ACRT(d) and TWFE diagnostics.

open_problems.open_problem: Is the finite-grid Lipschitz/isotonic ACRT(d) nuisance polytope law-sharp, or are there feasible ATT(l|d) arrays that cannot be realized by any joint potential-outcome panel law?

source_refs.excerpt: pointwise sharp sensitivity bounds may fail to paste across doses under the continuous marginal sensitivity model
law-level joint sharpness is refuted by the density-version obstruction
A genuine latent-law realization theorem would need a non-corner finite construction

open_problems.why_unsolved: Track A found a direct warning from a nearby continuous-dose shape-restriction proposal: pointwise sharp sensitivity bounds may not paste into one joint law. The ACRT(d) proposal can only claim sharpness if every feasible cross-dose ATT(l|d) array has a latent completion preserving observed panel margins, or if the envelope is explicitly downgraded to an outer bound.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: Either a finite latent-completion theorem mapping every feasible Lipschitz/isotonic ATT(l|d) grid into a full potential-outcome panel law, or a counterexample with a feasible LP point that violates joint law realizability.

writing_exemplar.why: It provides the closest general language for observational equivalence, random sets, and sharp identified-set construction.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: observed data restrict latent structures -> sharp identified sets require exact observational equivalence -> ACRT(d) shape constraints need a law-level completion or obstruction

method_exemplars.technique: Random-set and observational-equivalence characterization of sharp identified sets.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The obstacle is not solving an LP but proving the LP feasible points correspond to realizable latent potential-outcome laws.

method_exemplars.technique: Set-valued latent-variable restrictions and consistency of identified-set estimators under interval data.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is structurally close to proving when latent completions induce the same observable implications as finite inequality restrictions.

open_problems.open_problem: Can continuous-treatment DiD ACRT(d) bounds be extended to no-stayer designs by using Lipschitz cross-dose extrapolation rather than point-identifying stayers or near-zero-dose controls?

source_refs.claim: Continuous-treatment DiD with no stayers requires alternatives to TWFE because TWFE relies on linearity or homogeneous-effect restrictions that are not robust to heterogeneity.
source_refs.author: Clement de Chaisemartin, Xavier D'Haultfoeuille, Gonzalo Vazquez-Bare

source_refs.claim: When treatments are continuously distributed in every period, switcher-based DiD estimators identify averages of potential-outcome slopes under placebo-testable parallel trends.
source_refs.author: Clement de Chaisemartin, Xavier D'Haultfoeuille, Felix Pasquier, Doulo Sow, Gonzalo Vazquez-Bare

open_problems.why_unsolved: No-stayer continuous-treatment designs are precisely where cross-dose counterfactuals are most exposed, but the current literature separates robust switcher/no-stayer estimators from shape-restricted partial identification. It does not characterize the ACRT(d) identified interval when missing stayer information is replaced by a joint Lipschitz or isotonic cross-dose extrapolation set.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A no-stayer identification-and-bounds result defining the observed contrasts, replacing unavailable stayer controls with Lipschitz/isotonic cross-dose inequalities, and proving the resulting ACRT(d) endpoints are sharp LP values.

writing_exemplar.why: It is the closest recent motivation arc for continuous-treatment DiD when no stayer group exists.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: many policies are continuous -> TWFE is not robust under heterogeneous effects -> no-stayer designs need alternatives, including shape-restricted partial bounds

method_exemplars.technique: Switcher-based nonparametric continuous-treatment DiD identification under placebo-testable parallel trends.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The no-stayer extension must state exactly which observed switcher contrasts remain usable before shape-restricted extrapolation begins.

method_exemplars.technique: Projection inference for functions of partially identified parameters under moment inequalities.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The no-stayer ACRT(d) endpoint remains a projected functional of a partially identified finite-dimensional vector.

literature_map:
1. Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna (2021) is the anchor: continuous-treatment DiD identifies treated-type parameters but cross-dose comparisons and TWFE coefficients are difficult under heterogeneity.
2. de Chaisemartin-D'Haultfoeuille and coauthors provide adjacent continuous-treatment DiD designs for switchers, treatments continuously distributed at every period, and no-stayer cases.
3. D'Haultfoeuille-Hoderlein-Sasaki (2021) gives a repeated-cross-section continuous-treatment DiD route under stationarity and rank restrictions, but not cross-dose shape-restricted partial ID.
4. TWFE decomposition papers diagnose nonconvex or contaminated regression weights; they do not turn those decompositions into a joint ACRT(d) partial-ID feasible set.
5. Manski-Pepper monotone restrictions are the foundational shape-restriction template for shrinking nonparametric treatment-effect bounds.
6. Ban-Kedagni and Ishihara show that monotone or related restrictions can tighten nonparametric or nonseparable identified sets.
7. Chernozhukov-Fernandez-Val-Hahn-Newey supplies the panel/nonseparability warning that linear fixed effects need not recover identified average effects.
8. Chernozhukov-Lee-Rosen frames bounds as optimized intersection bounds, close to the desired support-function view.
9. Kaido-Molinari-Stoye and Belloni-Bugni-Chernozhukov supply inference machinery for projections, subvectors, and many moment inequalities.
10. Stoye addresses scalar interval inference when sample analog intervals are empty or awkward, which is directly relevant to ACRT(d) endpoint inference.
11. Voronin (2025) is the closest LP-specific partial-identification reference for sharp bounds as LP values and inference under solution multiplicity.
12. Armstrong-Kolesar is a useful adjacent inference exemplar for linear functionals over convex smoothness or shape classes.
13. The missing niche is a sharp cross-dose-coupled Lipschitz/isotonic ACRT(d) identified set with L*(d), nonseparability witnesses, law-level sharpness, and plug-in endpoint inference.
prior_proposal_map:
Exact qid pid_contdid_acrt_lipschitz has only init state/pipeline artifacts; no existing proposal or reviews were found.
Most direct continuous-dose substrate: pid_dose_response_iv_v1, with finite LP envelope, adjacent-rank sign certificate, rho threshold, and reviewer requests for explicit two-bin witnesses.
Closest cross-dose regularity warning: pid_dose_response_shape_v1, where pointwise sensitivity plus monotone-concave LP survived but law-level joint sharpness/pasting failed.
Closest DID support-function substrate: pid_did_anticipation_bounded_v1, accepted bounded-anticipation ATT intervals with shared-coordinate aggregation.
Closest DID weight/frontier substrate: q_lpdid_bjs_frontier_v1, with unproved timing-share frontier, signed divergence, and plug-in diagnostic.
Closest latent-realization/inference substrate: pid_proximal_l2_bridge_v1, with interval endpoints, realization caveats, and regime-wise endpoint inference question.
A failed factorial-DID angle supplied a useful support-budget sign-reversal witness pattern.
Overall Track-A gap: no artifact directly couples Callaway-Goodman-Bacon-Sant'Anna cross-dose selection terms into a joint Lipschitz/isotonic ACRT(d) nuisance set.
